tasks.title: Retrieve relevant documents and policies on Booking.com no-show and cancellation disputes
Search for recent cases and guidelines on Booking.com no-show complaints and guest cancellation claims
Analyze gathered information to identify steps for complaint triage and resolution
Outline actionable next steps for complaint handling and communication with property and guest
Compile a summary report of findings and recommended complaint triage actions

rag_output.summary:
- Support must verify the timeline when a property marks a reservation as no-show after the guest claims cancellation; any timeline conflicts should be escalated [1].  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show, support should consider the charge potentially refundable and gather cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner details before responding [2].  
- Support should determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or property); if the property charged directly, support must collect payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before proceeding [3].  

Sources: [1] refunds_policy.md, [2] refunds_policy.md, [3] payment_disputes.md

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: When the property marked the reservation as a no-show after the guest claims to have cancelled, support must verify the timeline before declining the request. Timeline conflicts should be escalated.

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the guest cancelled inside the free-cancellation window and the booking was not later marked as a no-show, support should treat the charge as potentially refundable and gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner before responding.

evidence.title: payment_disputes.md
evidence.excerpt: The first question is who collected the money: Booking.com or the property. If the property charged the guest directly, support should collect the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before deciding on next steps.

web_output.summary:
Task: Reporting guests as no-shows and requesting removal of guest reviews on Booking.com

Public evidence:
- Booking.com provides a process for properties to report guests as no-shows, which involves submitting relevant information through the partner platform to handle overbookings and no-shows effectively [1].
- Properties can request the removal of guest reviews if they violate Booking.com’s content guidelines, by submitting a removal request through the partner help center with justification [2].
- Both processes are designed to maintain accurate reservation records and ensure review integrity on the Booking.com platform [1][2].

Sources: [1] https://partner.booking.com/en-gb/help/reservations/overbookings-no-shows/reporting-guests-no-shows-your-property, [2] https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/guest-reviews/general/how-request-removal-guest-review

evidence.title: Reporting guests no-shows at your property - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: You can mark the booking as a no-show. This prevents guests from automatically receiving a satisfaction survey when they didn't stay with you.

evidence.title: How to request the removal of a guest review - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: The reservation is marked as a no-show and the review doesn't detail the guest's experience; You canceled the booking on the planned day of ...

evidence.title: Customer terms of service
evidence.excerpt: 2. If you cancel a booking or don't show up, any cancellation/no-show fee and any refund will depend on the Service Provider's cancellation/ ...

action_plan:
- Verify the exact timeline of the guest’s cancellation and when the property marked the booking as no-show; escalate if there are timeline conflicts or discrepancies [internal 1].  
- Determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or property); if the property charged directly, request payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation timeline from the property to assess refund eligibility [internal 3].  
- If cancellation was within the free-cancellation window and no-show marking appears incorrect, consider the charge potentially refundable; communicate with the property via the partner platform to correct the reservation status and, if applicable, guide them on requesting removal of any guest reviews violating guidelines [internal 2; public 1,2].
next_steps:
1. Request and verify the guest’s cancellation timestamp and the exact time the property marked the booking as no-show; identify any timeline discrepancies.  
2. Confirm who processed the payment—Booking.com or the property—and if the property charged directly, obtain the payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation details from the property.  
3. If cancellation occurred within the free-cancellation window and the no-show marking seems incorrect, flag the charge as potentially refundable and prepare to communicate with the property.  
4. Contact the property via the partner platform to correct the reservation status if needed and advise on submitting a guest review removal request if applicable.
final_answer:
Issue:  
A property marked a reservation as a no-show after the guest claims they cancelled the booking. There is a need to verify the accuracy of the no-show status and assess refund eligibility.

Internal policy:  
- Support must verify the timeline of the guest’s cancellation versus when the property marked the booking as no-show; escalate if there are timeline conflicts [1].  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show correctly, the charge may be refundable. Support should collect the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment ownership details before responding [2].  
- It is essential to determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or the property). If the property charged directly, support must obtain the payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation timeline from the property before proceeding [3].

Public policy:  
- Properties can report guests as no-shows through the partner platform to manage overbookings and no-shows [1].  
- Properties may request removal of guest reviews that violate Booking.com’s content guidelines by submitting a removal request with justification via the partner help center [2].  
- These processes ensure accurate reservation records and maintain review integrity on Booking.com [1][2].

Recommended action:  
- Verify the exact timeline of the guest’s cancellation and when the property marked the booking as no-show. Escalate if discrepancies arise.  
- Confirm who processed the payment. If the property charged directly, request the payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation timeline from the property.  
- If cancellation was within the free-cancellation window and the no-show marking appears incorrect, consider the charge potentially refundable. Communicate with the property through the partner platform to correct the reservation status.  
- If applicable, guide the property on how to request removal of any guest reviews that violate content guidelines.

Next steps:  
1. Request and verify the guest’s cancellation timestamp and the time the property marked the booking as no-show; identify any timeline conflicts.  
2. Confirm payment processing party; if the property charged directly, obtain payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation details from the property.  
3. If cancellation was timely and no-show marking is incorrect, flag the charge as potentially refundable and prepare to engage with the property.  
4. Contact the property via the partner platform to correct the reservation status and advise on submitting a guest review removal request if necessary.

Cited Sources:
- https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/guest-reviews/general/how-request-removal-guest-review

- /Users/ganesamaniankolappan/Documents/Temp_work/Agent/data/rag/payment_disputes.md
- https://partner.booking.com/en-gb/help/reservations/overbookings-no-shows/reporting-guests-no-shows-your-property
- https://www.booking.com/content/terms.html
